title: rubia peregrina
intro: Rubia peregrina, also known as the wild madder or woodland madder, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stain tissues and its potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Rubia peregrina include its use in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to act as a natural dye. Herbal preparations of Rubia peregrina can be made from the plant's roots and stems, which are typically dried and then used to create teas, infusions, or tinctures.
